earnings_surprise_pead.summary: Post-Earnings Announcement Drift (PEAD): Bernard & Thomas (1989) found stocks continue to drift in the direction of earnings surprise for 60 days post-announcement. Effect is concentrated in smaller, less-followed companies where information asymmetry is highest. Academic consensus: PEAD is strongest in small-cap stocks (Q1-Q2 of market cap), weakest or absent in large-caps. Arbitrage by institutions has reduced the effect significantly since 1990s. By 2010s, the effect is largely arbitraged away for S&P 500 mega-caps.
sources: Bernard & Thomas (1989) - original PEAD paper
Fama (1998) - Market efficiency critique noting PEAD survives but is small
Chordia et al (2009) - Liquidity and PEAD: more liquid stocks have less drift
Own backtest 2026-03-19: 33 mega-cap S&P 500 events, 2020-2024
earnings_surprise_pead.key_finding: PEAD is NOT present for mega-cap S&P 500 companies with >10% EPS beats. Our backtest: discovery set (2021-2022) shows +1.05% avg abnormal 1d return, 45% positive rate. 1d-to-5d drift: -0.94% (mean reversion, not drift). Financials (GS/MS) show similar: +0.40% 1d, then -1.50% 1d→5d drift. 2023-2024 tech shows large returns (+8.37%) but confounded by AI theme (NVDA) and META profitability turnaround - not general PEAD.
earnings_surprise_pead.regime_note: 2023-2024 period shows strong positive returns after tech beats, but these are driven by fundamental regime changes (AI adoption, META cost discipline) rather than generic PEAD. NVDA May 2023 (+23.5% abnormal) represents first AI guidance beat - one-time structural shift. These cannot be generalized as a repeatable pattern.

sp500_index_addition.summary: The S&P 500 inclusion effect: stocks added to the S&P 500 experience abnormal positive returns around the announcement and before the effective date. Mechanism: forced buying by passive index funds creates predictable demand. Classic effect documented by Shleifer (1986), Harris & Gurel (1986). Chen et al (2004) find avg CAR of +6% from announcement to effective date (permanent effect). However, Greenwood & Sammon (2025, JF) document the 'disappearing index effect' - abnormal returns collapsed from ~7.4% (1990s) to <1% (2010-2020) as the additions became predictable and markets more efficient.
sources: Shleifer (1986) JF - Do Demand Curves for Stocks Slope Down?
Harris & Gurel (1986) JF - Price and Volume Effects with S&P 500 Changes
Chen, Noronha & Singal (2004) JF - Price Response to S&P 500 Additions and Deletions
Greenwood & Sammon (2025) JF - The Disappearing Index Effect (NBER WP)
Own backtest 2026-03-19: 25 S&P 500 additions, 2020-2024
sp500_index_addition.key_finding: OWN BACKTEST FINDS SIGNIFICANT EFFECT in 2020-2024 despite G&S finding effect disappeared by 2020. Discovery (2020-2022, n=15): 1d abnormal +4.09%, 5d abnormal +5.52%, 73% positive rate. Validation (2023-2024, n=10): 1d abnormal +8.05%, 5d abnormal +10.23%, 100% positive rate. POSSIBLE CONFOUNDERS: TSLA (EV theme), MRNA (COVID vaccine), SMCI/PLTR (AI theme) may inflate results. Effect still visible when these are analyzed separately. KEY INSIGHT: Effect may be strongest when (a) stock is not from S&P MidCap 400 (more surprising), (b) stock has thematic tailwinds at time of addition.
sp500_index_addition.regime_note: Effect observed in 2020-2024 during QE/stimulus era and AI bull market. May not persist in bear market or rising rate environments. Monitor validation carefully.
sp500_index_addition.post_effective_reversal_note: POST-EFFECTIVE-DATE REVERSAL: NOT PRESENT. Own backtest 2026-03-19, N=24 events (2020-2024). Measured from effective date: 1d avg=-0.61% (p=0.36), 5d avg=-0.41% (p=0.59), 20d avg=+1.16% (p=0.70). No significant reversal at any horizon. Gains from announcement window are PERMANENT (investor recognition). Supports Chen et al. (2004) permanent effect over price pressure hypothesis. The 5-day hold in H-061ae3a8 is safe; gains do not reverse after effective date.
sp500_index_addition.live_validation_march_2026: MARCH 2026 LIVE DATA: VRT +8.46%, LITE +13.85%, COHR +6.17%, SATS +2.59% abnormal 1d. Average: +7.77% 1d, +7.38% 5d, 100% positive rate (4/4). Announcement: March 6 2026 after hours. All 4 additions positive - confirms H-061ae3a8 effect is alive. 5-day returns already captured by ~March 13.

fda_decision.summary:
FDA PDUFA drug approval/rejection effects on stock prices. Effect is strongly 
SIZE-DEPENDENT: large pharma approval = ~+0.4% Day 0. Small biotech approval = +8-11% abnormal. 
ASYMMETRY: Rejection (CRL) causes -4.3% to -9.2% (3-day CAR literature), much larger than positive on approval.
First-cycle approval rate at PDUFA: ~75-85%. Post-rejection drift: continued negative drift for 2+ weeks.
CRITICAL DISCOVERY: CMC-only CRLs show very different stock reactions than clinical/efficacy CRLs.
sources: PMC9439234 (2022) - 13,807 clinical trial outcomes, 2000-2020
PMC10817120 (2024) - How news affects biopharma stock prices
Muralitharan & Banerjee (SSRN 6027774, 2025) - FDA CRL reactions by deficiency type
Own backtest 2026-03-19: 8 small biotech CRL events 2018-2025 (4 delistings unmeasurable)

key_findings.biotech_vs_pharma_gap: +8-11% higher for small biotech vs large pharma
key_findings.biotech_stdev: 4.59% per literature, but own backtest shows 41-46% stdev (extreme outliers)
key_findings.crl_deficiency_type: CMC CRLs cause 5.4pp less damage than clinical CRLs (Muralitharan 2025)

own_backtest_2026.survivorship_bias: HIGH - 4/12 events are delisted. These are the most impactful cases (SESN -80%). True average is MORE negative than measured.
own_backtest_2026.clinical_crl_results: OTLK 2023: -81.3% 1d; APLT 2024: -76.9% 1d; NERV 2024: +1.2% 1d (pre-anticipated); TRVN 2018: -6.9% 1d; ITRM 2021: +17.8% 1d (market expected worse)
own_backtest_2026.vrca: VRCA 2022: -64.7% 1d (3rd CRL for same drug = extreme punishment)

actionable_patterns: 1. CMC-only CRL = NOT actionable for short (market correctly identifies as fixable)
2. Clinical deficiency CRL for SINGLE-ASSET small biotech (<$500M mkt cap) = potentially LARGE short (OTLK -81%, APLT -77%)
3. Clinical CRL when market expected approval (pre-PDUFA run-up) = larger crash
4. Pre-anticipated clinical CRL (stock already down) = near-zero or positive reaction (NERV +1.2%)
fda_decision.reason_not_hypothesis: Cannot form hypothesis due to: (1) data quality >30% failure from delistings, (2) extreme variance (stdev 41%), (3) critical CMC vs clinical distinction unresolved in sample, (4) need single-asset company filter to reduce variance. Need alternative price data for delisted tickers.
fda_decision.next_steps: Find historical price data for SESN, ATNX, ICPT via SEC Edgar or alternative sources. Classify ALL events by CMC vs clinical deficiency type. Filter for single-asset companies. Need N=15+ clinical CRL events to overcome variance.

insider_buying_cluster.summary: Full-population VIX regime and cluster size interaction analysis. N=1566 (2021-2025). Key finding: VIX 25-30 signal is NOT uniform -- it depends critically on cluster size. For n>=6 clusters: VIX 25-30 shows +2.37% mean, 57% positive rate, p=0.027 -- TRADEABLE. For n<6 clusters in VIX 25-30: -0.48% mean, 38% positive rate, p=0.68 -- DO NOT TRADE. Previous finding of VIX<20 being the single threshold is now SUPERSEDED by this interaction model.
sources: Own analysis 2026-03-22: cluster_with_returns.csv N=1566 2021-2025
insider_buying_cluster.gaps: Discrepancy between full-population 2021-2022 results (negative) vs original sampled backtest (positive +2.93%) needs resolution. Possible causes: (1) original used open-entry vs current close-entry, (2) different event filtering, (3) original used sector adjustment not just SPY. Critical to resolve before lowering confidence in 2021-2022 era.

activist_investor_13d.summary: Activist investor 13D filing date (public SC 13D filing) as signal: Tested Starboard Value 13D initial filings N=24 (2020-2024). 1d avg=+0.26%, 3d avg=+0.30%, 5d avg=-0.17%. Positive rate 45.8%. Does NOT pass multiple testing. Signal has fully decayed from Brav et al (2008) +7% finding. Reason: 10-day filing window means markets discover position during accumulation (news leaks), so the 13D filing date is NOT the information event.
activist_investor_13d.known_magnitude: Near zero in 2020-2024 (1d=+0.26%, p=N/A). Literature found +7% for 2001-2006.
activist_investor_13d.known_timeframe: N/A - no signal at 1d-20d horizons for 13D filing date
sources: Brav, Jiang, Partnoy & Thomas (2008) JF: +7% abnormal in [-20,+20] window around 13D filing
Greenwood & Schor (2009): only M&A-targeting activists show persistent long-run gains
Own backtest 2026-03-21: Starboard Value N=24 initial 13D filings 2020-2024
activist_investor_13d.gaps: Signal may recover if using actual first-news-coverage date instead of SEC filing date. Requires Bloomberg/Refinitiv news database to access. The causal mechanism remains valid - activists do create value - but we cannot access the true announcement date without premium news data.

fda_crl_clinical_post_crash.summary: Academic research (Investor underreaction to FDA CRL news): Long-term market reactions to FDA Phase III clinical trial announcements (ScienceDirect 2025) found evidence of investor underreaction - prices continue to drift in the same direction after initial crash. Larger reactions for small companies. CMC deficiencies associated with ~5pp less negative abnormal return than clinical deficiencies (ResearchGate academic paper on CRL deficiency types). This supports the post-gap continuation drift hypothesis.
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash.known_magnitude: 3-event sample: avg 3d abnormal = -19.35%, avg 5d abnormal = -30.27% (vs benchmark/sector). All 3 events showed continuation (0% positive rate at 3d).
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash.known_timeframe: Continuation signal peaks at 3-5 days. Entry: short from crash-day open. Exit: 3-5 days later.
sources: https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1544612325013947 - Long-term market reactions to FDA Phase III clinical trial announcements
https://www.researchgate.net/publication/399488839_Stock_Market_Reactions_to_FDA_Complete_Response_Letters_Evidence_from_Deficiency_Types_Firm_Characteristics_and_Tail_Risk
FDA CRL database: https://api.fda.gov/transparency/crl.json (419 total CRLs)
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash.gaps: N=3 events insufficient for statistical test (Wilcoxon p=N/A). Need 3+ more qualifying events. Key gap: most qualifying events are delisted (SESN, TCDA, YMAB etc) and data not available via Tiingo. Events from 2019-2020 era especially hard to verify.

fda_crl_clinical_short.summary: FDA CRL clinical-primary crash continuation signal: N=5 events (ARDX Jul 2021, GRTX Aug 2023, OTLK Aug 2023, NERV Feb 2024, APLT Nov 2024) ALL show SHORT continuation from crash-day open. 0% positive rate. Avg 3d abnormal=-19.83%, avg 5d=-29.34%. Reversal pattern also identified: (1) 2nd+ CRL for same company, (2) safety-primary CRL, (3) diversified pipeline, (4) pre-anticipated (stock down >20% in 30d before), (5) device/usability CRL.
fda_crl_clinical_short.gaps: N=5 insufficient for OOS split (need 3+ validation instances = N>=7). Cannot formally pre-register. Looking for 2+ more events. Main bottleneck: delisted tickers (SESN, TCDA, FGEN, YMAB all delisted with no data). FBRX Sep 2021 was phase 2 failure (not CRL) - different category.

clinical_trial_failure_continuation.summary: Phase 2/3 trial failures cause 55%+ crashes. Continuation signal exists but is HETEROGENEOUS (67% continuation, 33% reversal). Key discriminator appears to be whether the company has a viable path forward.
clinical_trial_failure_continuation.known_magnitude: -20 to -46% abnormal 3d for continuation events; +12 to +34% for reversal events
sources: Backtested 9 events: FBRX, AVIR, PRQR, ATHX, ANTX, NMRA, RZLT, GOSS, IMMP (2021-2026)

continuation_events.FBRX (2021-09-02): abn_3d=-26.0%, drug worse than placebo, no path, filed bankrupcty
continuation_events.PRQR (2022-02-10): abn_3d=-24.3%, all trial arms equal, halted program
continuation_events.ATHX (2022-05-19): abn_3d=-41.4%, filed Chapter 11, company wound down
continuation_events.ANTX (2024-02-11): abn_3d=-27.2%, Phase 3 halted, company went private
continuation_events.GOSS (2026-02-22): abn_3d=-37.2%, missed p-value threshold by hair (0.032 vs 0.025)
continuation_events.IMMP (2026-03-12): abn_3d=-26.2%, lead program discontinued, TACTI-003 remains

reversal_events.AVIR (2021-10-18): abn_3d=+12.4%, Roche partnership, other COVID assets, viable path
reversal_events.NMRA (2025-01-01): abn_3d=+30.3%, first of 3 planned Phase 3s, M+ cash, rational bet on remaining trials
reversal_events.RZLT (2025-12-10): abn_3d=+33.5%, ersodetug - company had cash runway + alternative indications possible

clinical_trial_failure_continuation.gaps: Need to quantify the discriminating factor (cash runway, number of pipeline assets, prior program failures). Cannot yet predict continuation vs reversal systematically. Recommend collecting cash position + pipeline count for each event.

clinical_efficacy_failure_short.summary: Single-pipeline small biotech stocks that crash >55% on clinical efficacy failure (Phase 2/3 or FDA CRL) continue to decline -27% on average over 3 trading days. N=11, 100% directional accuracy at 3d/5d, p=0.001. Two event types share the same causal mechanism: removal of all projected revenue for a single-drug company. Registered as hypothesis d302c84b.
sources: Bhattacharya et al. (2009): Phase 2/3 failures cause -8 to -11% 3-day CAR for small biotech
Hwang (2013): FDA CRL rejections cause persistent post-announcement drift
Our own N=11 backtest 2019-2026 (both CRL and Phase 2/3 combined)
clinical_efficacy_failure_short.gaps: Need more events to test >$500M cap boundary (ARDX was larger), need to test in high-VIX regimes

sec_wells_notice.summary: SEC Wells Notice disclosure effect (when companies announce receipt of Wells Notice via 8-K). Tested 22 events 2020-2024. 1d avg=-1.95% (p=0.0196), 3d avg=-2.05% (p=0.1934). Only 1d passes p<0.05 but fails Bonferroni requirement. 1d positive rate: 31.8% (directional but weak). SNR=0.55. High variance (stdev 3.62% at 1d). Not actionable.
sources: Own backtest 2026-03-21: 22 Wells Notice 8-K events 2020-2024
EDGAR full-text search: SC 13D with Wells Notice language
sec_wells_notice.gaps: Heterogeneous event type - procedural Wells notices (NDAQ, XYL, HAYW) show near-zero reaction. Would need news database to classify fraud vs procedural Wells notices.

ceo_sudden_departure_short.summary: Surveyed 20+ large-cap CEO/CFO sudden departures 2019-2024. Stock reactions are HIGHLY MIXED — some negative, some positive. Key insight: departures where CEO was fired FOR CAUSE (scandals, misconduct, performance failure) tend to be negative on announcement; departures where market sees the CEO as the problem (relief rally) are positive. A blanket short signal is NOT supported. Need to segment by departure reason.
ceo_sudden_departure_short.known_magnitude: Ranges from -19% (GME Furlong) to +8% (DIS Iger return) on announcement day. Abnormal returns vary widely.
sources: CNBC, CNN, Bloomberg coverage of individual events 2019-2024
LevelFields AI: CEO Departures and Stock Price Impact
Paragon Intel: What a CFO Departure Historically Means for Public Company Performance
ceo_sudden_departure_short.gaps: Need to segment by: (1) fired for cause vs resigned under pressure vs planned; (2) whether stock was already in freefall; (3) company size. Also need systematic EDGAR 8-K Item 5.02 search for comprehensive dataset.

small_cap_first_buyback.summary: First-ever buyback announcements by small-caps ($100M-$2B) show no reliable short-run abnormal return. N=8 clean events, all horizons fail multiple testing. The Ikenberry 1995 effect holds for ALL repurchases but cannot be isolated for first-ever events due to sample size and contamination constraints.
sources: Ikenberry, Lakonishok, Vermaelen (1995) - JFQA: 3-3.5% short-run, 12.1% 4-year CAR for all open-market repurchases. Effect concentrated in value stocks.
small_cap_first_buyback.gaps: Broader open-market repurchase signal in small-cap value stocks untested (N=50+ possible). First-ever buyback structurally hard to isolate at scale.

analyst_coverage_initiation.summary: Analyst coverage initiation (first 'buy' or 'strong buy' rating from major broker-dealer) generates positive abnormal returns. Literature: Irvine (2003) found +3.8% cumulative abnormal returns in 14 days pre-announcement (information leakage) and +1.2% on announcement day itself for initiations with 'buy' recommendation. Barber et al (2001): upgrades (from hold to buy) generate +0.35% day 0, but effect has decayed. Key problem: data availability - consensus analyst data typically requires Bloomberg/FactSet paid API. EDGAR does not contain analyst ratings. Cannot backtest without paid data source.
analyst_coverage_initiation.known_magnitude: +1-4% on initiation day for 'buy' recommendations; effect stronger for small/mid-cap stocks with few analysts
analyst_coverage_initiation.known_timeframe: Primarily day-0 to day+1 for intraday effect; post-announcement drift weaker than PEAD
sources: Irvine (2003) JF: Analyst Forecasts and Brokerage-Firm Trading
Barber et al (2001) JF: Can Investors Profit from the Prophets
analyst_coverage_initiation.gaps: Cannot backtest without paid analyst data (Bloomberg/Refinitiv). Free data sources do not have reliable analyst initiation dates. Signal likely decayed in liquid large-caps but may persist in mid-cap coverage initiations.

insider_buying_cluster_5d.description: 2+ corporate insiders making open-market purchases >$50K within 30 days produces significant 3-5 day abnormal returns. Tested on 2020-2022 SEC Form 4 data.
insider_buying_cluster_5d.statistical_significance: p<0.000001 in both discovery and OOS
insider_buying_cluster_5d.oos_verdict: PASS — OOS stronger than discovery (bear market regime)
insider_buying_cluster_5d.regime_dependence: Possible: 2022 bear market OOS shows stronger signal than 2020-2021 bull/mixed discovery. Hypothesis: insider conviction buying during broad declines carries more information. Need 2023-2024 bull market data to confirm regime dependence.
insider_buying_cluster_5d.survivorship_bias: 35% average data failure rate (delisted tickers). M&A targets (positive) and bankruptcies (negative) both missing. Net direction unknown.
insider_buying_cluster_5d.filter_criteria: 2+ insiders, >$50K each, within 30-day window, Form 4 filing date
insider_buying_cluster_5d.data_source: SEC Form 4 bulk data (edgar.sec.gov structured data sets)

insider_buying_cluster_cluster_size.description: CLUSTER SIZE MATTERS: 3+ insider clusters show markedly stronger signal than 2-insider minimum.
insider_buying_cluster_cluster_size.oos_2022_3plus: 1d avg +3.47%, 80% positive rate, p<0.0001; 5d avg +4.85%, 71% positive
insider_buying_cluster_cluster_size.mechanism: 3+ filter reduces 10b5-1 pre-planned transaction noise; more signalers = more conviction
insider_buying_cluster_cluster_size.recommendation: For live trading, prefer 3+ insider clusters over 2-insider minimum

insider_buying_cluster.finding: n=6-9 insider cluster tier is regime-robust; n>=10 has ZERO signal
insider_buying_cluster.n10plus_explanation: n>=10 events in 2021 are dominated by IPO lockup expiry Form 4 filings (CXM, BRZE, IOT, GLUE, DOCS, DNUT etc.). These are NOT voluntary open-market purchases. Even n=6-9 was contaminated in 2021 by IPO bubble. Signal is clean post-2021.
insider_buying_cluster.trading_rule: For n=6-9 clusters: trade in BOTH calm and elevated VIX (p<0.005 in both). For n=3-5: only trade in calm VIX (p=0.27 in elevated = coin flip). Never trade n>=10.

insider_buying_cluster_3plus.effect: 3+ insider cluster buys within 30 days: abnormal returns at 3d and 5d consistently positive
insider_buying_cluster_3plus.1d_signal_decay: 1d weakened in 2023-24: 55.3% pos (vs 72-80% in 2021-22). 3d hold more robust.
insider_buying_cluster_3plus.best_hold_period: 3 trading days (optimal by Sharpe: high pos_rate, strong median)
insider_buying_cluster_3plus.signal_direction: LONG at open day after last cluster filing
insider_buying_cluster_3plus.bias_note: Favorable survivorship bias (acquisitions >> bankruptcies in delisted pool)

insider_buying_cluster_largecap.effect: LARGECAP VALIDATION: 3+ insider cluster signal holds when filtered to large-cap stocks
insider_buying_cluster_largecap.interpretation: Signal is NOT driven by micro-cap outliers. Effect persists for S&P500-scale companies (>5B market cap). Median returns more reliable (less skewness). Confirms trading GO at ~$5B is within the validated signal range.
insider_buying_cluster_largecap.bias_note: Largecap filter reduces survivorship bias - most >500M companies have continuous price history. Only 6/272 events failed (2.2% failure rate vs 40% full universe)

fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.example: ALDX March 17 2026 (SECOND CRL — reproxalap for dry eye disease)
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.event: FDA CRL mid-label-negotiation — FDA issued label drafts Dec 2025 AND Mar 2026, then issued CRL same month. Company stated "label negotiations were not completed" — genuinely unexpected.
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.pre_drift: Stock down ~25% in prior 10 days, but this may NOT be CRL anticipation — likely cash runway concerns (prior CRL noted cash into "late 2025") and sector weakness rather than CRL-specific knowledge
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.first_crl: Nov 27 2023 — required additional trial. Company ran additional trial, resubmitted NDA.
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.second_crl: Mar 17 2026 — FDA rejected again: "lack of substantial evidence", "inconsistency of study results", "totality of evidence does not support effectiveness". No safety/manufacturing issues. FDA did NOT recommend additional trials — effectively a final rejection.
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.key_signal_for_scanner: Active label draft negotiations (FDA providing label drafts) followed by CRL = unexpected event. Pre-crash drift alone is NOT sufficient to disqualify as pre-anticipated — need to check whether company was in active dialogue with FDA.
fda_crl_clinical_post_crash_drift.tradeable: Would have been difficult given pre-drift, but the -70.7% on a $109M market cap stock on a day with active label negotiations makes this a strong example of the fda_clinical_rejection_short signal

fda_crl_clinical_short.finding: Repeat CRL rejections are NOT tradeable with the standard first-time-CRL short hypothesis
fda_crl_clinical_short.recommendation: Add screening criterion: exclude repeat CRLs (CRL #2 or higher). Only first-time clinical efficacy failures qualify.

fda_clinical_rejection_short.finding: Repeat CRL rejections (2nd or 3rd) behave differently from first-time CRLs
fda_clinical_rejection_short.evidence: ALDX (3rd CRL, Mar 17 2026): +19.73% abnormal 1d; ATRA (2nd CRL, Jan 10 2026): +4.47% day 1 then -19.93% by 3d
fda_clinical_rejection_short.interpretation: Repeat CRLs are already priced in — market may bounce on certainty reduction. First-time CRL from clinical efficacy failure is the reliable signal.
fda_clinical_rejection_short.discriminating_factor: Is this the FIRST clinical efficacy failure for this drug? If so, signal applies. If repeat rejection, do NOT trade.

fda_crl_clinical_short_DEPRECATED.note: DEPRECATED - event dates were wrong in previous sessions. Use fda_clinical_rejection_short instead.

wrong_dates.APLT: 2024-02-14 (wrong - was CMC CRL resubmission positive) → correct: 2024-11-27

clinical_trial_failure_recent_2026.note: Both show continuation. Both are 2026 events, potentially tradeable in future.

clinical_efficacy_failure_short.goss_reversal_case: GOSS Feb 23 2026 (PROSERA Phase 3 miss): REVERSAL EXCEPTION. Entry at open Feb 24 (0.388): 1d=+4.40%, 3d=+1.55%, 5d=+9.67%. Bounce mechanism: company simultaneously announced strong subgroup data (intermediate/high-risk PAH p=0.021) and FDA Type C meeting plans. Market priced ongoing regulatory path. Stock: 0.388->0.58 by March 4 (+49%). Disqualifying factor: stated FDA path forward (analogous to AVIR/Roche). Criterion 6 added to hypothesis: company must have NO stated regulatory path forward.

sp500_index_addition.finding: March 2026 Q1 rebalance live validation (4 new events post-hypothesis creation)
sp500_index_addition.note: We missed this trade (watchlist only had June 2026). Added to backtest evidence. Confirms signal strength in Q1 2026 market (AI infrastructure theme: VRT/COHR/LITE all AI-adjacent).

vwap_breakout_momentum.status: preliminary - needs larger universe and regime filtering before hypothesis
vwap_breakout_momentum.constraint: net returns after ~0.40% round-trip costs may be below 1% minimum threshold at shorter horizons

ceo_performance_failure_departure_short.finding: GPOR (Mar 9, 2026): CEO departure confirmed signal. Close-entry abnormal 1d = -7.68%. Open-entry abnormal 1d = -3.11% (better than average but gap was missed). 5d reversal to +3.03% (signal fades by day 5).
ceo_performance_failure_departure_short.interpretation: GPOR is a missed trade that supports the gap-entry mechanism. Open entry still shows -3.11% which is directionally correct and above the 0.5% threshold, unlike the average of -0.14% for the full N=12 sample.
ceo_performance_failure_departure_short.note: GPOR may be a special case — oil & gas CEO departure may have different dynamics than tech/retail. Do not add to formal hypothesis without checking sector-specific patterns.

ceo_performance_departure.finding: Signal fires entirely in overnight gap, not intraday
ceo_performance_departure.gap_contribution: avg -3.11% of total -3.25% effect is in overnight gap

adbe_validation.note: CEO Narayen step-down amid AI competition

ceo_performance_departure.actionability_conclusion: Signal requires after-hours entry (post-announcement, pre-gap) to capture. Intraday-only accounts cannot trade this signal.

insider_cluster_6_to_9.description: OPTIMAL CLUSTER SIZE TIER: 6-9 insiders is the uniquely regime-robust tier
insider_cluster_6_to_9.discovery_2021_2022: 3d avg=+3.48%, pos_rate=69.7%, p<0.000001, N=132 measured (216 attempted, 39% failure)
insider_cluster_6_to_9.validation_2023_2025: 3d avg=+4.13%, pos_rate=64.6%, p<0.000001, N=178 measured (<1% failure)
insider_cluster_6_to_9.regime_robustness: Works in BOTH calm VIX<20 (p=0.0004) AND elevated VIX 20-30 (p=0.027). EV ranges from +5.70% (VIX<15) to +2.37% (VIX 25-30). ONLY cluster tier significant across regimes.
insider_cluster_6_to_9.comparison_to_other_tiers: n=3-5: fails in elevated VIX (p=0.27). n>=10: noise (p=0.64, 49% pos_rate). n=6-9: optimal signal/noise ratio.

insider_buying_cluster_n6to9.effect: n=6-9 cluster 3d abnormal returns: 2023-2025 N=179 avg=+4.53% pos=67% p<0.0001; 2021-2022 N=133 avg=+0.77% pos=53% p=0.23 -- NO SIGNAL
insider_buying_cluster_n6to9.note: The n=6-9 signal is ONLY statistically significant in 2023-2025 data. The 2021-2022 period shows no signal, likely contaminated by SPAC-era insider mechanics and micro-cap noise. Full 2021-2025 population (N=353) passes multiple testing at 3d/5d/10d but this is driven by the 2023-2025 subset.
insider_buying_cluster_n6to9.implication: Hypothesis 416556df pre-registered with 2021-2022 as 'discovery' but this period shows no signal. The true discovery is 2023-2025. This means we lack a proper pre-2023 discovery period. Treat hypothesis as exploratory until a proper temporal OOS split is available with 2026+ data.
insider_buying_cluster_n6to9.discovery_backtest: All 2021-2025: N=353, 3d avg=+2.39% p<0.0001, 5d avg=+3.72% p<0.0001, passes_multiple_testing=True
insider_buying_cluster_n6to9.by_period: 2021-2022: N=133, 3d avg=+0.77% p=0.23, NOT significant; 2022-only: N=56, p=0.16 NOT significant; 2023-2025: N=179, 3d avg=+4.53% p<0.0001, STRONG signal

dead_ends.reason: Tested PEAD on 33 S&P 500 large-cap events (2020-2024): NVDA, AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, META (tech), GS, MS (financials). Discovery set (2021-2022): avg 1d abnormal +1.05%, positive rate 45% (below random!), 1d→5d drift = -0.94% (mean reversion, not drift). Financial sector (GS/MS, 2020-2021): avg 1d = +0.40%, 1d→5d drift = -1.50%. No statistical significance at any horizon. Does not pass multiple testing correction. Below min_abnormal_return_pct threshold (1.5%). PEAD literature concentrated in small/mid-cap. Mega-caps are too efficiently priced — institutions arbitrage away within hours. EXCEPTION: The 2023-2024 period shows large returns for NVDA and META, but these are thematic regime changes (AI adoption, profitability turnarounds), not general PEAD. Do NOT pursue general PEAD for mega-cap S&P 500 companies.

Tested S&P 500 deletion effect (SHORT when stock removed from index), N=21 events (2021-2026).
Overall results: 1d avg=-0.44% (pos%=52%, p=0.59 - random), 3d avg=-1.99% (pos%=19%, p=0.0078 - significant),
5d avg=-1.23% (pos%=48%, p=0.27 - not significant). Technically passes multiple testing overall (3d at p<0.01 Bonferroni).
BUT: OUT-OF-SAMPLE VALIDATION FAILS. Validation set (2024-2026, N=13): 1d avg=+0.27% (POSITIVE!), 3d avg=-2.14%
(p=0.015, does NOT pass Bonferroni p<0.01 threshold). The 1d return is +0.27% in validation (opposite direction!).
Many stocks RISE on day 1 after deletion (RHI +1.15%, BWA +2.80%, FMC +3.38%). Critical issue: SEVERE SELECTION BIAS -
stocks are removed because they've already been declining fundamentally. Cannot separate deletion effect from fundamental
deterioration. Pattern at 3d likely reflects continued fundamental selling, not index rebalancing mechanics. The 
'forced selling' by index funds appears to be offset by contrarian buyers on D+1, with the net effect nearly flat.
DO NOT pursue deletion SHORT hypothesis. The addition effect is clearly alive; the deletion SHORT is not actionable.

Tested generic 'SHORT small biotech after any CRL' hypothesis. N=8 measured (4 delisted = survivorship bias).
Results: 1d avg=-25.24% (median=-2.84%), 5d avg=-28.51%, positive_rate_1d=50%. Does NOT pass multiple testing (no horizons significant at p<0.05, though p=0.12 at 1d with N=8). 
CRITICAL ISSUES: (1) CMC-only CRLs show POSITIVE 1d returns (AXSM +4.8%, SRRK +4.1%) - wrongly shorting these would lose money. (2) Clinical CRLs have EXTREME variance - OTLK -81% but ITRM +18%, NERV +1.2%. (3) 33% data failure from delistings = survivorship bias. 
DO NOT pursue generic CRL short. The effect is real but too heterogeneous. Need CONDITIONAL sub-hypothesis: (single-asset company) + (clinical deficiency CRL) + (pre-announcement run-up, suggesting market expected approval). That specific sub-case might be consistently very negative, but requires 15+ events with that profile.

Mid-cap PEAD ($5-30B market cap, >10% EPS beats) does not exist. N=19 events across WING/TREX/IBP/OLLI/FIVE: 1d avg=+0.01%, 20d avg=+1.27%, all p-values 0.67-0.99, 50% positive rate at 20d = essentially random. Per-symbol: IBP +11.99% vs OLLI -12.94% — no consistent direction. Confirms academic finding that PEAD is primarily a microcap phenomenon. Mean reversion and noise dominate at mid-cap due to better analyst coverage and institutional ownership.

Pre-ex-dividend run-up for high-yield S&P 500 stocks (VZ/MO/T/PM/KMI, 2021-2024) does NOT produce a tradeable signal. Tested 3 windows: 5-day (3d p=0.122), 20-day (20d p=0.158), 4-day pre-ex (3d p=0.223). None pass multiple testing. OOS validation: discovery 0.60% vs validation 0.21% — shrinking effect. Per-symbol inconsistency: VZ +1.29%, T +0.88% vs MO -0.19%, KMI -0.22% (3d abnormal). Effect is real academically (Hartzmark & Solomon 2013: +41 bps/month) but ~0.4% average is below our 1.5% minimum threshold and transaction costs would eliminate it. Not actionable.

Phase 2/3 trial failures are too heterogeneous without filtering: 67% continuation, 33% reversal. Does not pass multiple testing (pos_rate=33%, avg_3d=-11.78%). Need discriminating factor: company cash runway, pipeline depth, drug credibility. Continuation cases: company wound down, drug clearly worthless. Reversal cases: company has other programs, cash to retry, partner support.

Backtested N=10 mid-cap CEO/CFO sudden departure events (2022-2024). avg_abnormal_3d=+0.19%, positive_rate_3d=40%, passes_multiple_testing=False. Signal too noisy for 3-5 day hold. Events are idiosyncratic - LCID CFO departure (Dec 2023) showed +12.8% 3d reversal; FRPT CFO departure showed +11.2% 3d reversal. No discriminating factor found to separate continuation from reversal cases. Academic literature shows this as a 1-3 month signal, not 3-5 day. Would need 30+ day hold and different strategy. Not tradeable at our 3-5 day horizon with current methodology.

Attempted to backtest reverse split announcement signal. N=3 (CLOV, NKLA, MVIS) shows avg_abnormal_1d=+3.2% (POSITIVE, unexpected), avg_abnormal_3d=-1.57%, positive_rate_3d=33%. Signal does not exist in 3-5 day post-announcement window. Main problems: (1) Most qualifying NASDAQ-compliance reverse splits involve micro-cap companies (<M market cap) with poor liquidity and high borrow costs. (2) Post-2023 NASDAQ rules require only 2 business days notice so announcement and effective date are simultaneous. (3) Signal is 12-month drift per academic literature (Desai & Jain 1997), not 3-5 day. (4) The 1d reaction is slightly positive (artificial demand from share consolidation). Not tradeable at 3-5 day horizon.

Wells Notice disclosures (SEC 8-K): Tested 22 events (2020-2024). 1d avg=-1.95% (p=0.0196), 3d avg=-2.05% (p=0.1934). Only 1 horizon passes at p<0.05 - fails Bonferroni requirement (need p<0.01). 1d positive rate 31.8% (68.2% negative) shows directional tendency but SNR=0.55 is too low. High variance: stdev 3.62% at 1d. COIN May 2023 is outlier (+18% 3d) due to crypto industry dynamics. Large-cap sub-sample (N=16, ex-COIN): 1d p=0.04, 3d p=0.09 - still fails multiple testing. Core issue: Wells Notices are heterogeneous (fraud vs procedural) and the procedural ones (NDAQ, XYL, HAYW) show near-zero reaction. Would need N=50+ to sub-classify reliably. NOT WORTH PURSUING without access to legal case classification.

Activist investor SC 13D filing date as signal: Tested Starboard Value initial 13D filings (N=24, 2020-2024). 1d avg=+0.26%, 3d avg=+0.30%, 5d avg=-0.17%. Positive rate 45.8% (below 50%). Does not pass multiple testing. MECHANISM FAILURE: SC 13D filings have a 10-day filing window after crossing 5% threshold. News of activist accumulation typically leaks before the official filing date, so the filing date is NOT the information event. The market reprices on news discovery (often during accumulation) rather than at filing. Brav et al (2008) found +7% for 2001-2006 data - this effect has DECAYED completely in 2020-2024. Could potentially recover signal using news discovery dates (first media coverage) rather than filing dates, but would require Bloomberg/Refinitiv news database. Causal mechanism still valid but signal access requires news data we do not have.

Large-cap 8-K Item 4.02 filings show avg 1d abnormal return of -0.30% (heterogeneous, 67% positive rate). Three fundamental problems: (1) 8-K filing date is NOT the announcement date for most events - market often learns via earnings calls, press releases, or trading halts before the 8-K is filed; (2) Direction is ambiguous - some restatements are upward revisions (RGEN, LZ, TRU all went UP); (3) 2021 SPAC warrant restatements contaminate the dataset. PRCH showed -16% on actual-announcement-day but this is exceptional. N=12 large-cap events, passes_multiple_testing=False. Signal is not reliable enough to trade.

Large buyback announcements (>5% market cap) as a signal have been eroded by market efficiency post-2007. Academic literature (multiple studies 2020-2024) confirms long-horizon abnormal returns are near zero for US large/mid-caps. Short-term 3d CAR is ~1.4-1.6% but barely clears transaction costs and is not confirmed for US market 2020-2024. Counter-evidence: Salesforce first-ever B buyback (Aug 2022) caused -10% reaction because investors read it as low-growth signal. The general signal is dead. A narrower sub-hypothesis may have merit: first-ever programs at small-caps (<B mcap) buying back >10% of float. Not yet tested.

IG-to-junk credit downgrades tested as short signal (3-5d hold). DEAD END for 3 reasons: (1) Insufficient clean events: most fallen angel events cluster in 2020 COVID crisis making it impossible to isolate the downgrade effect from market-wide selloff. Only 4-5 clean non-crisis events identifiable in 2018-2024 (KHC 2019, GE 2021, DISH 2019, VFC 2023). Insufficient for OOS split (need min 6). (2) Pre-anticipation: fallen angel downgrades are typically preceded by months of credit watch periods. Stock market prices in the downgrade well before the announcement. GE Oct 2018 downgrade day saw +7% stock return (relief rally, not crash). VFC Nov 2023 downgrade day coincided with earnings beat, up +10%. (3) Wrong mechanism for equity alpha: credit downgrades affect bond prices directly. The equity effect requires forced selling by IG-only bond funds (which does not affect stock price directly) and higher cost of capital repricing (too slow for 3-5d hold). Literature: Kliger & Sarig (2000) found credit rating changes affect bond markets more than equity markets. Equity abnormal returns on rating change day are near zero.

Companies issuing intra-quarter profit warnings / negative guidance revisions via 8-K Item 7.01. Tested 6 events 2019-2022 (SNAP, AMD, AAPL, FDX, TGT, WMT). Results: 1d avg abnormal=-0.86%, 3d avg=-7.84% (driven by SNAP outlier), p=0.311-0.629. FAILS multiple testing. Heterogeneous outcomes: SNAP shows -41.2% 3d continuation (extreme -43% initial crash), but AAPL, TGT, FDX show near-zero or POSITIVE 3d abnormal. Core problem: mega-cap profit warnings attract fast-reacting institutional buyers (buy-the-dip); only micro/small-cap warnings with >30% initial crash might show continuation. But those events are hard to find systematically via EDGAR. Decision: dead end for systematic trading. The d302c84b hypothesis already captures the best version of this pattern (biotech-specific, very large crashes).

Dividend cuts/suspensions at historically reliable dividend payers tested as short signal. Tested 8 non-COVID events 2018-2024 (GE, INTC, WBA, VFC, KHC, PCG, CTL, MPW). Results: 1d avg abnormal=-1.09% (p=0.55), 3d avg=-7.92% (p=0.147). Fails multiple testing. High variance: MPW +8.4% (investors interpreted cut as positive for balance sheet), VFC +6.5% 3d (recovery), KHC 3d movement driven by concurrent $15B goodwill writedown not dividend. PCG driven by bankruptcy context, not dividend. Core issue: dividend cuts are almost always concurrent with other negative news (earnings misses, restructuring) making it impossible to isolate dividend effect. Also many are pre-anticipated (stock already in decline). Income-focused REIT/utility stocks (MPW) have different investor base that views dividend cut as discipline signal. NOT actionable as standalone signal.

IPO lock-up expiry (typically 180 days post-IPO) tested as short signal. Tested 12 events 2020-2022. Results: 1d avg=+0.12% (flat), 3d avg=+1.66% (positive). Fails multiple testing. 50% positive rate = random. Key issues: (1) Effect has been pre-arbitraged - sophisticated traders SHORT before expiry, creating price pressure weeks before the event date, then BUY on the expiry date itself. This means the expiry DATE shows mean reversion (positive), not continuation (negative). (2) Heterogeneous outcomes: RIVN -5.7%, BRZE -4.6% vs DOCS +3.7%, MNDY +7.2%, COIN +1.9%. (3) Exact lock-up date uncertain - varies by share class, can be extended. Academic literature (Field & Hanka 2001): -2% at expiry in 1990s has likely decayed. NOT actionable.

SPAC merger announcement day effect (De-SPAC announcement): tested 5 events 2020-2021. 1d avg abnormal=-0.38% (p=0.92), 1d positive rate=40%. Direction entirely heterogeneous: IPOB +12.4%, CCIV -8.0% (buy-rumor-sell-news, leaked weeks before), DCRB -6.7%, PSTH -2.0%, GGPI +2.3%. Fails multiple testing. Key problems: (1) LEAK contamination - many SPAC targets were widely rumored before official announcement (CCIV pre-ran +400% before Feb 22 announcement). (2) REGIME contamination - Q4 2020/Q1 2021 SPAC bubble: mean SPAC price day after announcement was $15.77 (from $10 base), but by Q2 2021 fell back to ~$10 with no consistent jumps (Kiesel 2023, European Financial Management). (3) Survivorship/delistment: 3 of 8 events (VTIQ, SHLL, AJAX) unmeasurable due to delisting. (4) No sub-filter improves signal: long SPAC (momentum) or short SPAC (dilution realization) both fail. Academic finding: SPACs that closed in Q4 2020/Q1 2021 had mean price of $9.01 by Dec 2021 (-43% from announcement high). The announcement DAY return is not a tradeable signal - neither direction is consistent.

Stock split announcement effect: tested 11 events 2020-2024. 1d avg=-0.84%, pos_rate=36.4% (NEGATIVE). 3d avg=+2.06%, pos_rate=54.5%, p=0.27. Fails multiple testing. Main issues: (1) Almost all large-cap split announcements are confounded with quarterly earnings - the earnings surprise effect dominates any split signal (NVDA 2024 +11.6% 3d was earnings-driven, DECK -7.9% 1d was earnings miss). (2) The few standalone splits (PANW, TSCO) showed -2.4% and -0.4% at 1d. (3) Even TSLA Aug 2020 (+4.4% 1d) may have been momentum-driven rather than split-driven. (4) Literature finding of +2.5% abnormal return for split announcements likely applies to non-confounded mid-cap events; for large-caps with simultaneous earnings, earnings dominate. Alternative path: test mid-cap standalone stock splits (split announcement NOT on earnings day, market cap $500M-$5B). These would be harder to find but less confounded.

Tested 20 large-cap stocks 2018-2023 (n=489 events). Hypothesis: stocks trading >15% above 1-year rolling VWAP face selling pressure from investors with unrealized gains (disposition effect). REJECTED: Stocks above VWAP 15% consistently OUTPERFORM over all horizons 1-20 days (abnormal +0.64% to +0.86%, all p<0.01). Low-volume filter (n=363) also shows positive returns, contradicting the specific mechanism. High-volume breakouts show strongest momentum (+0.87% to +1.19%). Signal is the opposite direction — momentum, not mean reversion. OOS 2024 (n=120) confirms. See tools/vwap_deviation_signal.py.

Tested March 21 2026. Hypothesis: stocks rising on LOW volume have more unrealized gains among existing investors -> selling pressure -> bearish. HIGH volume rise -> more turnover -> new holders -> less selling -> bullish. Results: Directional intuition PARTIALLY REVERSED. High-turnover (volume >1.2x normal during 60d rise) underperforms at 5d (-0.04% to -0.54%) but OUTPERFORMS at 20d (+1.83%). Low-turnover rises show +0.22% to +1.22% across horizons. FAILS multiple testing correction: only 1 horizon significant at p<0.05 for each group (need 2+ or 1 at p<0.01). Signal cannot be distinguished from noise. The MOMENTUM effect (stocks already rising 10%+ tend to keep rising) dominates any disposition selling pressure. Volume during the rise adds no meaningful predictive power on top of price momentum. For a precise cost-basis computation (MVRV ratio), tick-level data or intraday order flow data is required — not available with yfinance daily OHLCV. Recommendation: Do not pursue as standalone signal. Might be usable as a weak confirmation signal but evidence insufficient.

DEEPER TEST March 21 2026: Previous session (small universe) found +0.64-1.19% at 1d for stocks >15% above 252d VWAP. Re-tested with 100-stock universe, N=14,412 observations (2014-2024), monthly sampling. Results: 1d abnormal = -0.08% (p<0.0001, NEGATIVE and significant), 3d = ~0%, 5d = +0.05 to +0.19%, 10d = +0.25 to +0.52%. Higher thresholds (20-30%) do not improve signal meaningfully. Calm VIX filter makes results WORSE (more negative at 1-5d). With N=14,412 the signal-to-noise ratio is clear: the short-term VWAP breakout momentum does NOT exist. What does exist: slight mean reversion at 1d (statistically significant but economically tiny at -0.08%). The 10d drift of +0.25-0.52% is real but far too small to trade profitably after costs (min threshold 1.5%). CONCLUSION: Do not pursue VWAP breakout as a standalone trading signal. Previous session finding was small-sample noise.

Tested March 21 2026. Hypothesis: stocks with high % of past-year volume below current price (many holders in profit) face selling pressure -> lower forward returns. Method: computed percent_in_profit using daily OHLCV volume profile over 252-day lookback. Universe: 50 large-cap S&P 500 stocks, 2021-2023. Results: HIGH PIP (>75%, N=18761): mean abnormal return=+0.05%, pos%=50.3%, p=0.016 (positive direction, NOT negative as predicted). LOW PIP (<35%, N=8972): mean=+0.07%, pos%=50.5%. High vs Low PIP difference: -0.01%, p=0.73 (no significant difference). Multi-horizon: 1d=0%, 5d=+0.09%, 20d=+0.36% - all POSITIVE (momentum, not reversal). The disposition effect does NOT aggregate to produce market-level selling pressure in large-cap stocks. This is the 4th test of related signals (VWAP overextension, volume-disposition, VWAP momentum, volume profile PIP) - all dead ends. The mechanism (individual investor disposition effect) exists at micro level but is dominated by momentum forces and institutional investor behavior at market level. NOT worth further testing for large-cap stocks.

Tested March 21 2026. N=9 large/mid-cap events (AAPL, FDX, TGT, WMT, MU, SNAP, NKE, INTC), 2018-2024. Results: avg_1d=-0.71% (pos_rate=56%), avg_5d=+0.11% - essentially random. passes_multiple_testing=False. HIGH HETEROGENEITY: blue chips (WMT +5.6%, TGT +2.5%, FDX +1.8% at 1d) BOUNCE after guidance cuts (buy-the-dip dynamic, quality floor). Growth/weaker companies (SNAP -11.0%) continue falling. Entry at next-day open captures post-gap drift which is mostly positive for blue chips. KEY INSIGHT: Mega-cap guidance cuts are ALREADY in the price before open - market efficiently prices the cut within the after-hours session. No tradeable drift. Potential sub-hypothesis: SHORT guidance cuts for high-growth stocks (EV/EBITDA >30) where the valuation depends on future earnings - SNAP-like situations. But N is very small and SNAP was an extreme outlier (-43% vs -11% benchmark-adjusted). DEAD END for general guidance cut signal. Similar finding to profit_warning_preannouncement dead end from earlier session.

CEO relief rally (market celebrates bad CEO removal) → SHORT at next-day open does NOT work. N=12 expanded test shows avg 10d = -1.34%, p=0.5693. Original N=8 result (-5.47% at 10d) was small-sample noise. Individual events highly variable: DG +13.73%, KSS +8.92%, SBUX 2024 +0.39%, GE +4.57% vs PTON -19.64%, WFC -6.80%. No consistent pattern.

Steer.md question (March 2026 session): tested 4 variations of volume/cost basis disposition effect signal. All dead ends. (1) Volume-adjusted VWAP deviation: p_5d=0.027 fails multiple testing. (2) VWAP breakout LONG 100-stock universe N=14,412: real 1d=-0.08% mean reversion. (3) PIP profile cost basis HIGH vs LOW: difference=-0.01% p=0.73. (4) CEO relief rally as disposition-effect proxy: dead end. The core concept — stocks with large unrealized gains facing more selling pressure — is NOT measurable/exploitable at the large-cap level in practice. Institutional momentum dominates.

Tested N=8 clean first-ever buyback events (2018-2024). No horizon passed p<0.05. Bootstrap CI includes zero at all horizons. Structural issues: (1) earnings contamination on same day makes clean isolation hard, (2) sample size capped ~15-20 due to rarity, well below N=32 power requirement, (3) heterogeneous motives (financial strength vs price support). Ikenberry et al 1995 covers ALL repurchases and shows 3% short-run effect, but that evidence does not hold for isolated first-ever small-cap events. Possible follow-on: broader open-market repurchase signal in small-cap value stocks (not first-ever) could reach N=50+ for testing.

VWAP_252 breakout signal (Close/VWAP >= 1.15): tested N=489 events across 20 large-cap stocks (2018-2023) with OOS 2024 confirmation. Best abnormal return was +1.19% at 20d, which is below the min_abnormal_return_pct threshold of 1.5%. Signal direction is correct (momentum, not mean-reversion as hypothesized) but magnitude is too small to trade. Regime filtering (calm VIX) and high-volume filter both tested; neither brings any horizon above 1.5% abnormal. Conclusion: signal is real but not large enough to be tradeable within current methodology constraints. No hypothesis formed.
